title: Germany Data Residency Baseline (GDPR + BDSG)
notes: Constrains placement to German cloud regions and evidences GDPR plus the German Bundesdatenschutzgesetz (BDSG). Suited to public-sector, healthcare and DACH enterprise workloads that must remain in-country. Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not a cryptographic data-path proof.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to a German region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed German cloud region (Frankfurt/Berlin) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: Placement outside Germany can move data abroad; German public-sector and regulated workloads frequently require in-country residency.

articles.title: Scope of the Federal Data Protection Act

rules.name: No unrestricted or extra-territorial egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress can move personal data outside Germany without a transfer safeguard.

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the controller (not only the provider) controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys reduce provider access to plaintext and strengthen the sovereignty posture.

articles.title: Requirements for the security of data processing

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is a baseline technical measure under the security-of-processing obligation.

articles.title: Requirements for the security of data processing
